x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces DEP's open environmental reference data (reservoir levels, water consumption, water quality, green infrastructure, hydrants, permits) and adds report_water_problem — the net-new write workflow today split between NYC311 and the login-walled uMAX My DEP Account portal.

server.name: io.nyc.dep
server.title: NYC Environmental Protection (DEP)
server.description: Agent-native access to the New York City Department of Environmental Protection — reservoir levels and citywide water consumption, harbor/drinking-water/watershed/lead-copper water quality, green-infrastructure assets, hydrants, and water/air/asbestos permits from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to report and track a water or sewer problem (water service request) that today is split between NYC311 and the uMAX billing portal.
server.instructions: Use find_reservoir_levels for water-supply state and find_water_consumption for citywide usage. find_water_quality searches samples across DEP's programs (Harbor, Drinking Water Distribution, Watershed, Lead and Copper) — analyte values live in the sample's 'measurements' map and may be comparator strings like '<1' because DEP publishes many analytes as free text. find_green_infrastructure / get_green_infrastructure cover stormwater assets; find_hydrants / get_hydrant cover distribution assets; find_permits covers water/sewer, clean-air (CATS), and asbestos permits. report_water_problem (net-new) creates a water/sewer service request; it mutates state — confirm the location, category, and description with the user before submitting, and set emergency=true for water-main breaks, sewer backups into a home, flooding, or no water so it routes to DEP's 24-hour customer service. list_my_service_requests and get_service_request read a customer's own tickets.

tools.name: find_reservoir_levels
tools.title: Find Reservoir Levels
tools.description: List reservoir-level observations across the NYC water-supply system by reservoir, system (Catskill/Delaware/Croton), or time window.

tools.name: find_water_consumption
tools.title: Find Water Consumption
tools.description: Return citywide water-consumption figures (population, million gallons per day, per-capita) by year. Consumption is published only at the citywide aggregate; per-account usage lives in the uMAX portal.

tools.name: find_water_quality
tools.title: Find Water Quality
tools.description: Search water-quality samples across DEP monitoring programs (Harbor, Drinking Water Distribution, Watershed, Lead and Copper) by program, site, borough, or date. Analyte readings are returned in each sample's 'measurements' map.

tools.name: find_green_infrastructure
tools.title: Find Green Infrastructure
tools.description: Search DEP green-infrastructure stormwater assets (rain gardens, bioswales, porous pavement) by borough, asset type, status, or council district.

tools.name: get_green_infrastructure
tools.title: Get Green Infrastructure Asset
tools.description: Fetch a single DEP green-infrastructure asset by its GI_ID.

tools.name: find_hydrants
tools.title: Find Hydrants
tools.description: Search public fire hydrants in DEP's water-distribution network by borough or community board.

tools.name: get_hydrant
tools.title: Get Hydrant
tools.description: Fetch a single hydrant by its UNITID.

tools.name: find_permits
tools.title: Find Permits
tools.description: Search DEP water/sewer, clean-air (CATS), and asbestos (ACP7) permits by permit type, status, borough, or tax block/lot.

tools.name: list_my_service_requests
tools.title: List My Service Requests
tools.description: List the authenticated customer's own DEP water/sewer service requests and their status — today split between NYC311 and the uMAX My DEP Account portal.

tools.name: get_service_request
tools.title: Get Service Request
tools.description: Fetch a single water/sewer service request by its id.

tools.name: report_water_problem
tools.title: Report a Water Problem
tools.description: Create a DEP water/sewer service request — report a water-main break, no water, a leak, a sewer backup, a clogged catch basin, a hydrant problem, discolored water, or a billing/account issue. NET-NEW workflow with no single public API today — it is split between NYC311 and the login-walled uMAX My DEP Account portal. Mutates state — confirm location, category, and description first; set emergency=true for water-main breaks, sewer backups into a home, flooding, or no water so it routes to DEP's 24-hour customer service.
